name: Llama 3
summary: Meta's open flagship large language model.
description: Llama 3 is Meta's third-generation open LLM. It is released in 8B and 70B parameter sizes with a 128k vocabulary tokenizer, a 128k context window, and improved reasoning and instruction-following performance over Llama 2.

timeline.title: Llama 3 released
timeline.description: Open weights for 8B and 70B parameter chat and base models.

timeline.title: Llama 3.1 405B
timeline.description: Frontier-class open weights released under a permissive license.

name: Mistral 7B
description: Mistral 7B demonstrated that a carefully trained 7B model could outperform the larger Llama 2 13B across reasoning, math, and code benchmarks. It popularized sliding-window attention and grouped-query attention in the open ecosystem.

timeline.title: Mistral 7B released
timeline.description: First Mistral open release, Apache 2.0 licensed.

timeline.title: Mistral 7B v0.2
timeline.description: Extended vocabulary and improved instruction following.

name: Stable Diffusion XL
summary: Two-stage text-to-image model with stunning fidelity.
description: Stable Diffusion XL (SDXL) combines a base UNet and a refiner UNet trained at 1024×1024 resolution. It dramatically improves photorealism, prompt adherence, and typography over the SD 1.5 baseline.

timeline.title: SDXL 1.0 released
timeline.description: Base + refiner pipeline, open weights under RAIL-M.
